content.claim: Hungary's early academic selection system tracks students at age 10-13 (Grade 5 or 7) into 6- or 8-year gimnázium tracks, earlier than the OECD average of age 15, with approximately 85% of Hungarian students in schools permitted to select by academic performance versus the OECD average of 43%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Figure reflects current policy structure but does not establish causal effects of early tracking on outcomes.
The 85% figure measures selection-capable schools, not actual selection intensity or decision-making practices.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
